SingleSelection.description: Whether the collection allows empty selection.
The currently selected key in the collection (controlled).
The initial selected key in the collection (uncontrolled).
Handler that is called when the selection changes.

MultipleSelection.description: The type of selection that is allowed in the collection.
Whether the collection allows empty selection.
The currently selected keys in the collection (controlled).
The initial selected keys in the collection (uncontrolled).
Handler that is called when the selection changes.
The currently disabled keys in the collection (controlled).

DragDropEvent.description: The x coordinate of the event, relative to the target element.
The y coordinate of the event, relative to the target element.

TextDropItem.description: The drag types available for this item. These are often mime types, but may be custom app-specific types.

FileDropItem.description: The file type (usually a mime type).

DropEvent.description: The drop operation that should occur.

ItemDropTarget.description: The drop position relative to the item.

DroppableCollectionInsertDropEvent.description: The drop operation that should occur.

DroppableCollectionRootDropEvent.description: The drop operation that should occur.

DroppableCollectionOnItemDropEvent.description: The drop operation that should occur.
Whether the drag originated within the same collection as the drop.

DroppableCollectionReorderEvent.description: The keys of the items that were reordered.
The drop operation that should occur.

DroppableCollectionUtilityOptions.description: The drag types that the droppable collection accepts. If the collection accepts directories, include `DIRECTORY_DRAG_TYPE` in your array of allowed types.
Handler that is called when external items are dropped "between" items.
Handler that is called when external items are dropped on the droppable collection's root.
Handler that is called when items are dropped "on" an item.

Handler that is called when items are reordered within the collection. This handler only allows dropping between items, not on items. It does not allow moving items to a different parent item within a tree.
Handler that is called when items are moved within the source collection. This handler allows dropping both on or between items, and items may be moved to a different parent item within a tree.
A function returning whether a given target in the droppable collection is a valid "on" drop target for the current drag types.

DroppableCollectionBaseProps.description: Handler that is called when a valid drag enters a drop target.
Handler that is called after a valid drag is held over a drop target for a period of time.
Handler that is called when a valid drag exits a drop target.
Handler that is called when a valid drag is dropped on a drop target. When defined, this overrides other drop handlers such as `onInsert`, and `onItemDrop`.

DroppableCollectionBaseProps.type: (target: DropTarget, types: DragTypes, allowedOperations: DropOperation[]) => DropOperation
DroppableCollectionBaseProps.description: A function returning the drop operation to be performed when items matching the given types are dropped on the drop target.

DraggableCollectionStartEvent.description: The keys of the items that were dragged.

DraggableCollectionMoveEvent.description: The keys of the items that were dragged.

DraggableCollectionEndEvent.description: The keys of the items that were dragged.
Whether the drop ended within the same collection as it originated.

DraggableCollectionProps.description: Handler that is called when a drag operation is started.
Handler that is called when the drag is moved.
Handler that is called when the drag operation is ended, either as a result of a drop or a cancellation.
A function that returns the items being dragged.
The ref of the element that will be rendered as the drag preview while dragging.
Function that returns the drop operations that are allowed for the dragged items. If not provided, all drop operations are allowed.

StyleProps.description: Sets the CSS [className](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/className) for the element. Only use as a **last resort**. Use style props instead.
Sets inline [style](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLElement/style) for the element. Only use as a **last resort**. Use style props instead.
The margin for all four sides of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/margin).
The margin for the logical start side of the element, depending on layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/margin-inline-start).
The margin for the logical end side of an element, depending on layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/margin-inline-end).
The margin for the top side of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/margin-top).
The margin for the bottom side of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/margin-bottom).
The margin for both the left and right sides of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/margin).
The margin for both the top and bottom sides of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/margin).
The width of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/width).
The height of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/height).
The minimum width of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/min-width).
The minimum height of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/min-height).
The maximum width of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/max-width).
The maximum height of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/max-height).
When used in a flex layout, specifies how the element will grow or shrink to fit the space available. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/flex).
When used in a flex layout, specifies how the element will grow to fit the space available. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/flex-grow).
When used in a flex layout, specifies how the element will shrink to fit the space available. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/flex-shrink).
When used in a flex layout, specifies the initial main size of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/flex-basis).
Specifies how the element is justified inside a flex or grid container. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/justify-self).
Overrides the `alignItems` property of a flex or grid container. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/align-self).
The layout order for the element within a flex or grid container. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/order).
When used in a grid layout, specifies the named grid area that the element should be placed in within the grid. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/grid-area).
When used in a grid layout, specifies the column the element should be placed in within the grid. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/grid-column).
When used in a grid layout, specifies the row the element should be placed in within the grid. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/grid-row).
When used in a grid layout, specifies the starting column to span within the grid. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/grid-column-start).
When used in a grid layout, specifies the ending column to span within the grid. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/grid-column-end).
When used in a grid layout, specifies the starting row to span within the grid. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/grid-row-start).
When used in a grid layout, specifies the ending row to span within the grid. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/grid-row-end).
Specifies how the element is positioned. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/position).
The stacking order for the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/z-index).
The top position for the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/top).
The bottom position for the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/bottom).
The logical start position for the element, depending on layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/inset-inline-start).
The logical end position for the element, depending on layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/inset-inline-end).
The left position for the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/left). Consider using `start` instead for RTL support.
The right position for the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/right). Consider using `start` instead for RTL support.
Hides the element.

ViewStyleProps.description: The Spectrum color token version number.
The background color for the element.
The width of the element's border on all four sides. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-width).
The width of the border on the logical start side, depending on the layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-inline-start-width).
The width of the border on the logical end side, depending on the layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-inline-end-width).
The width of the top border. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-top-width).
The width of the bottom border. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-bottom-width).
The width of the left and right borders. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-width).
The width of the top and bottom borders. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-width).
The color of the element's border on all four sides. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-color).
The color of the border on the logical start side, depending on the layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-inline-start-color).
The color of the border on the logical end side, depending on the layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-inline-end-color).
The color of the top border. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-top-color).
The color of the bottom border. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-bottom-color).
The color of the left and right borders. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-color).
The color of the top and bottom borders. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-width).
The border radius on all four sides of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-radius).
The border radius for the top start corner of the element, depending on the layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-start-start-radius).
The border radius for the top end corner of the element, depending on the layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-start-end-radius).
The border radius for the bottom start corner of the element, depending on the layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-end-start-radius).
The border radius for the bottom end corner of the element, depending on the layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/border-end-end-radius).
The padding for all four sides of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/padding).
The padding for the logical start side of the element, depending on layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/padding-inline-start).
The padding for the logical end side of an element, depending on layout direction. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/padding-inline-end).
The padding for the top side of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/padding-top).
The padding for the bottom side of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/padding-bottom).
The padding for both the left and right sides of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/padding).
The padding for both the top and bottom sides of the element. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/padding).
Species what to do when the element's content is too long to fit its size. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/overflow).

BoxAlignmentStyleProps.description: The distribution of space around items along the main axis. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/justify-content).
The distribution of space around child items along the cross axis. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/align-content).
The alignment of children within their container. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/align-items).
The space to display between both rows and columns. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/gap).
The space to display between columns. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/column-gap).
The space to display between rows. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/row-gap).

FlexStyleProps.description: The direction in which to layout children. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/flex-direction).
Whether to wrap items onto multiple lines. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/flex-wrap).

GridStyleProps.description: Defines the sizes of each row in the grid. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/grid-template-rows).
Defines the sizes of each column in the grid. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/grid-template-columns).
Defines the size of implicitly generated columns. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/grid-auto-columns).
Defines the size of implicitly generated rows. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/grid-auto-rows).
Controls how auto-placed items are flowed into the grid. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/grid-auto-flow).

GridStyleProps.type: Responsive<'auto' | 'normal' | 'start' | 'end' | 'center' | 'left' | 'right' | 'stretch' | 'self-start' | 'self-end' | 'baseline' | 'first baseline' | 'last baseline' | 'safe center' | 'unsafe center' | 'legacy right' | 'legacy left' | 'legacy center'>
GridStyleProps.description: Defines the default `justifySelf` for all items in the grid. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/justify-items).

AriaLabelingProps.description: Defines a string value that labels the current element.
Identifies the element (or elements) that labels the current element.
Identifies the element (or elements) that describes the object.
Identifies the element (or elements) that provide a detailed, extended description for the object.

AriaValidationProps.description: Identifies the element that provides an error message for the object.

FocusableDOMProps.description: Whether to exclude the element from the sequential tab order. If true, the element will not be focusable via the keyboard by tabbing. This should be avoided except in rare scenarios where an alternative means of accessing the element or its functionality via the keyboard is available.

TextInputDOMEvents.description: Handler that is called when the user copies text. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLElement/oncopy).
Handler that is called when the user cuts text. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLElement/oncut).
Handler that is called when the user pastes text. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLElement/onpaste).
Handler that is called when a text composition system starts a new text composition session. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/compositionstart_event).

Handler that is called when a text composition system completes or cancels the current text composition session. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/compositionend_event).
Handler that is called when a new character is received in the current text composition session. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/compositionupdate_event).
Handler that is called when text in the input is selected. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/select_event).
Handler that is called when the input value is about to be modified. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLElement/beforeinput_event).
Handler that is called when the input value is modified. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLElement/input_event).

InputDOMProps.description: The name of the input element, used when submitting an HTML form. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input#htmlattrdefname).

The `<form>` element to associate the input with. The value of this attribute must be the id of a `<form>` in the same document. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/input#form).

TextInputDOMProps.description: Describes the type of autocomplete functionality the input should provide if any. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input#htmlattrdefautocomplete).
The maximum number of characters supported by the input. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input#htmlattrdefmaxlength).
The minimum number of characters required by the input. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input#htmlattrdefminlength).
Regex pattern that the value of the input must match to be valid. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input#htmlattrdefpattern).
Content that appears in the input when it is empty. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input#htmlattrdefplaceholder).
The type of input to render. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input#htmlattrdeftype).

Hints at the type of data that might be entered by the user while editing the element or its contents. See [MDN](https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/interaction.html#input-modalities:-the-inputmode-attribute).

An attribute that takes as its value a space-separated string that describes what, if any, type of autocomplete functionality the input should provide. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input#autocomplete).
An enumerated attribute that defines whether the element may be checked for spelling errors. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Global_attributes/spellcheck).

LinkDOMProps.description: A URL to link to. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/a#href).
Hints at the human language of the linked URL. See[MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/a#hreflang).
The target window for the link. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/a#target).
The relationship between the linked resource and the current page. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Attributes/rel).
Causes the browser to download the linked URL. A string may be provided to suggest a file name. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/a#download).
A space-separated list of URLs to ping when the link is followed. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/a#ping).
How much of the referrer to send when following the link. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/a#referrerpolicy).
Options for the configured client side router.

FormProps.description: Validation errors for the form, typically returned by a server. This should be set to an object mapping from input names to errors.
Where to send the form-data when the form is submitted. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/form#action).
The enctype attribute specifies how the form-data should be encoded when submitting it to the server. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/form#enctype).
The HTTP method to submit the form with. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/form#method).

The target attribute specifies a name or a keyword that indicates where to display the response that is received after submitting the form. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/form#target).
Triggered when a user submits the form.
Triggered when a user resets the form.
Triggered for each invalid field when a user submits the form.
Indicates whether input elements can by default have their values automatically completed by the browser. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/form#autocomplete).
Controls whether inputted text is automatically capitalized and, if so, in what manner. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Global_attributes/autocapitalize).
An ARIA role override to apply to the form element.

Validation.description: Whether user input is required on the input before form submission.
Whether the input value is invalid.
Whether to use native HTML form validation to prevent form submission when the value is missing or invalid, or mark the field as required or invalid via ARIA.

A function that returns an error message if a given value is invalid. Validation errors are displayed to the user when the form is submitted if `validationBehavior="native"`. For realtime validation, use the `isInvalid` prop instead.

ValidationResult.description: Whether the input value is invalid.
The current error messages for the input if it is invalid, otherwise an empty array.
The native validation details for the input.

SpectrumFieldValidation.description: Whether the input should display its "valid" or "invalid" visual styling.

InputBase.description: Whether the input can be selected but not changed by the user.

ValueBase.description: Handler that is called when the value changes.

TextInputBase.description: Temporary text that occupies the text input when it is empty.

SpectrumTextInputBase.description: Temporary text that occupies the text input when it is empty. Please use help text instead.

RangeValue.description: The start value of the range.
The end value of the range.

RangeInputBase.description: The smallest value allowed for the input.
The largest value allowed for the input.
The amount that the input value changes with each increment or decrement "tick".

HelpTextProps.description: A description for the field. Provides a hint such as specific requirements for what to choose.
An error message for the field.

SpectrumHelpTextProps.description: Whether the description is displayed with lighter text.
Whether an error icon is rendered.

PressEvent.description: The type of press event being fired.
The pointer type that triggered the press event.
The target element of the press event.
Whether the shift keyboard modifier was held during the press event.
Whether the ctrl keyboard modifier was held during the press event.
Whether the meta keyboard modifier was held during the press event.
Whether the alt keyboard modifier was held during the press event.
X position relative to the target.
Y position relative to the target.
The key that triggered the press event, if it was triggered by a keyboard interaction. This is useful for differentiating between Space and Enter key presses.

LongPressEvent.description: The type of long press event being fired.

HoverEvent.description: The type of hover event being fired.
The pointer type that triggered the hover event.
The target element of the hover event.

KeyboardEvents.description: Handler that is called when a key is pressed.
Handler that is called when a key is released.

FocusEvents.description: Handler that is called when the element receives focus.
Handler that is called when the element loses focus.
Handler that is called when the element's focus status changes.

HoverEvents.description: Handler that is called when a hover interaction starts.
Handler that is called when a hover interaction ends.
Handler that is called when the hover state changes.

PressEvents.description: Handler that is called when the press is released over the target.
Handler that is called when a press interaction starts.
Handler that is called when a press interaction ends, either over the target or when the pointer leaves the target.
Handler that is called when the press state changes.
Handler that is called when a press is released over the target, regardless of whether it started on the target or not.

**Not recommended – use `onPress` instead.** `onClick` is an alias for `onPress` provided for compatibility with other libraries. `onPress` provides additional event details for non-mouse interactions.

FocusableProps.description: Whether the element should receive focus on render.

MoveStartEvent.description: The type of move event being fired.

MoveMoveEvent.description: The type of move event being fired.
The amount moved in the X direction since the last event.
The amount moved in the Y direction since the last event.

MoveEndEvent.description: The type of move event being fired.

MoveEvents.description: Handler that is called when a move interaction starts.
Handler that is called when the element is moved.
Handler that is called when a move interaction ends.

ScrollEvent.description: The amount moved in the X direction since the last event.
The amount moved in the Y direction since the last event.

ScrollEvents.description: Handler that is called when the scroll wheel moves.

ItemProps.description: Rendered contents of the item or child items.
Rendered contents of the item if `children` contains child items.
A string representation of the item's contents, used for features like typeahead.
An accessibility label for this item.
A list of child item objects. Used for dynamic collections.
Whether this item has children, even if not loaded yet.

AsyncLoadable.description: Whether the items are currently loading.
Handler that is called when more items should be loaded, e.g. while scrolling near the bottom.

SectionProps.description: Rendered contents of the section, e.g. a header.
An accessibility label for the section.
Static child items or a function to render children.

CollectionBase.description: The item keys that are disabled. These items cannot be selected, focused, or otherwise interacted with.

CollectionStateBase.description: A pre-constructed collection to use instead of building one from items and children.

Expandable.description: The currently expanded keys in the collection (controlled).
The initial expanded keys in the collection (uncontrolled).
Handler that is called when items are expanded or collapsed.

Sortable.description: The current sorted column and direction.
Handler that is called when the sorted column or direction changes.

SortDescriptor.description: The key of the column to sort by.

Collection.description: The item keys that are disabled. These items cannot be selected, focused, or otherwise interacted with.

Node.description: The type of item this node represents.
A unique key for the node.
The object value the node was created from.
The level of depth this node is at in the hierarchy.
Whether this item has children, even if not loaded yet.
The loaded children of this node.
The rendered contents of this node (e.g. JSX).
A string value for this node, used for features like typeahead.
An accessibility label for this node.
The index of this node within its parent.
A function that should be called to wrap the rendered node.
The key of the parent node.
The key of the node before this node.
The key of the node after this node.
The first child key of this node.
The last child key of this node.
Additional properties specific to a particular node type.
A function that renders this node to a React Element in the DOM.

LabelableProps.description: The content to display as the label.

SpectrumLabelableProps.description: The label's overall position relative to the element it is labeling.
The label's horizontal alignment relative to the element it is labeling.
Whether the required state should be shown as an icon or text.
Whether the label is labeling a required field or group.
A ContextualHelp element to place next to the label.

ButtonProps.description: The content to display in the button.

AriaBaseButtonProps.description: Indicates whether the element is disabled to users of assistive technology.
Indicates whether the element, or another grouping element it controls, is currently expanded or collapsed.
Indicates the availability and type of interactive popup element, such as menu or dialog, that can be triggered by an element.
Identifies the element (or elements) whose contents or presence are controlled by the current element.
Indicates the current "pressed" state of toggle buttons.
Indicates whether this element represents the current item within a container or set of related elements.
The behavior of the button when used in an HTML form.

Whether to prevent focus from moving to the button when pressing it. Caution, this can make the button inaccessible and should only be used when alternative keyboard interaction is provided, such as ComboBox's MenuTrigger or a NumberField's increment/decrement control.

The `<form>` element to associate the button with. The value of this attribute must be the id of a `<form>` in the same document. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/button#form).
The URL that processes the information submitted by the button. Overrides the action attribute of the button's form owner.
Indicates how to encode the form data that is submitted.
Indicates the HTTP method used to submit the form.
Indicates that the form is not to be validated when it is submitted.
Overrides the target attribute of the button's form owner.
Submitted as a pair with the button's value as part of the form data.
The value associated with the button's name when it's submitted with the form data.

AriaButtonElementTypeProps.description: The HTML element or React element used to render the button, e.g. 'div', 'a', or `RouterLink`.

LinkButtonProps.description: A URL to link to if elementType="a".
The target window for the link.
The relationship between the linked resource and the current page. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Attributes/rel).

ButtonAria.description: Whether the button is currently pressed.
